channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump administration cancels travel for refugees already cleared to resettle in the US
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Refugees who had been approved to travel to the United States before a Jan. 27 deadline suspending America's refugee resettlement program have had their travel plans canceled by the Trump administration. Thousands of refugees are now stranded at various locations around the globe.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI and REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump, GOP leaders meet at White House as president plans visit to NC, defends executive orders
item.description: President Donald Trump answered questions at a press availability on a range of topics following a White House meeting with top congressional Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump rescinds Biden's executive order on AI safety in attempt to diverge from his predecessor
item.description: Hours after returning to the White House, President Donald Trump made a symbolic mark on the future of artificial intelligence by repealing former President Joe Biden’s guardrails for the fast-developing technology.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump demands an apology from bishop who asked him to ‘have mercy’ on LGBTQ+ people and migrants
item.description: President Donald Trump is demanding an apology from the Episcopal bishop of Washington after she asked him to have mercy on the LGBTQ+ community and migrants in the United States illegally during a prayer service marking his inauguration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump's immigration crackdown leaves some families weighing the risk of sending kids to school
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — As President Donald Trump cracks down on immigrants in the U.S. illegally, some families are wondering if it is safe to send their children to school.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, MORIAH BALINGIT, BIANCA VÁZQUEZ TONESS and JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden left Trump 'inspirational' message in 'very nice' letter, new president says
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday described the letter former President Biden left him as “inspirational" and teased that he may release the note at some point.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pardons by Trump and Biden reveal distrust of each other and wobbly faith in criminal justice system
item.description: A day that began with the outgoing president’s pardon of lawmakers and his own family ended with the incoming president’s pardon of supporters who attacked the U.S. Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New Secretary of State Marco Rubio pauses refugee operations, ramps up visa vetting
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is advising his department to cease refugee resettlement operations and begin ramping up vetting of visas from certain regions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Presidents' unprecedented pardon power sparks scrutiny
item.description: The unprecedented use of pardons by former President Biden on his last day in office coupled with President Trump’s sweeping pardon of violent Jan. 6 offenders on his first has sparked renewed scrutiny of the singular power.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trump finds new ways to flex presidential power after returning to White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is swiftly breaching the traditional boundaries of presidential power as he returns to the White House, bringing to bear a lifetime of bending the limits in courthouses, boardrooms and politics to forge an expansive view of his authority.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Coast Guard surging assets to Gulf of America, other waterways to support Trump’s executive orders
item.description: In support of President Trump's executive orders, the Coast Guard will send additional resources to maritime borders, the military branch said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Minnesotans among those pardoned for Jan. 6 riots on President Trump’s first day
item.description: The president’s move halted a trial for two southern Minnesota men that had been underway in Washington D.C., and it erased the criminal convictions of another seven people who took part in the insurrection.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: Estimated 24.6 million TV viewers watched inauguration coverage, smallest audience since 2013
item.description: The Nielsen Company says an estimated 24.6 million people watched coverage of President Donald Trump's second inauguration.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Republican senators issue warning to historic museums ahead of pro-life march: ‘Respect’ free speech
item.description: Sens. Cruz and Young urge D.C. museums to respect First Amendment rights of pro-life protesters after Smithsonian kicked out students in 2023 for wearing pro-life gear.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Henderson mayor threatens legal action against councilwoman following ‘spurious rumors’ of extramarital affair
item.description: A litany of personal attacks has enveloped the city of Henderson, and elected officials are coming forward to explain why distrust has taken over a part of city hall.
item.dc:creator: James Schaeffer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Democrats struggle to pick their message against Trump's shock-and-awe campaign
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Democrats knew this was coming. President Donald Trump promised a shock-and-awe campaign to deliver major policy victories immediately after he took office. Much of it was outlined in the Project 2025 document that Democrats predicted he would adopt.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pastor Lorenzo Sewell says politics and spirituality intertwined
item.description: Sewell joined "NewsNation Now" to discuss the topic, contending there can be no spirituality without politics and vice versa.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration strips schools, churches of immigration enforcement protections
item.description: The memo rescinds a Biden administration guideline that created "protected areas" consisting of places where "children gather, disaster or emergency relief sites, and social services establishments.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo and Sergio Martínez-Beltrán, MPR News

item.title: Plan to reform 300 Nevada boards, commissions emerges
item.description: After Gov. Joe Lombardo took aim in his State of the State address at an ever-expanding number of boards and commissions that operate in Nevada with minimal oversight, a plan to reform the system is emerging.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Stefanik pledges an 'America First' agenda at the UN and a review of US funding
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Rep. Elise Stefanik pledged Tuesday to push President Donald Trump’s “America First” stance if confirmed as U.S. ambassador to the United Nations and roll out a review of U.S. funding critical for U.N. operations and its many agencies around the world. Noting that the U.S.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI and EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump’s federal health website scrubs ‘abortion’ search results
item.description: The most “relevant” results that come up in a search of “abortion” on HHS.gov, the website for the federal Department of Health and Human Services, are several years old, from the first Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin, MPR News

item.title: 22 states sue to stop Trump's order blocking birthright citizenship
item.description: Attorneys general from 22 states sued Tuesday to block President Donald Trump's move to end a century-old immigration practice known as birthright citizenship guaranteeing that U.S.-born children are citizens regardless of their parents’ status.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump cuts DEI regulations across federal government
item.description: In several executive orders signed Monday, Trump undid several Biden-era initiatives, saying they “end now.
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota and other states sue to stop Trump’s order blocking birthright citizenship
item.description: Attorneys general from 22 states have sued to block President Donald Trump’s move to end a century-old immigration policy known as birthright citizenship guaranteeing that U.S.-born children are citizens regardless of their parents’ status.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump demands an apology from bishop who asked him to ‘have mercy’ on LGBTQ+ people and migrants
item.description: President Donald Trump is demanding an apology from the Episcopal bishop of Washington after she asked him to have mercy on the LGBTQ+ community and migrants in the United States illegally during a prayer service marking his inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Tiffany Stanley And Gary Fields, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Supreme Court won’t take up fight over Pennsylvania mail ballot date requirement
item.description: The Supreme Court said Tuesday it won’t hear civil rights groups’ appeal seeking to invalidate Pennsylvania's dating requirement for mail ballots, which has invalidated thousands of votes each recent election and become a central legal fight over voting in the key swing state.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Trump is withdrawing from the Paris Agreement (again), reversing U.S. climate policy
item.description: President Donald Trump issued a barrage of orders Monday targeting Biden-era initiatives on global warming. The decisions come on the heels of the hottest year ever recorded.
item.dc:creator: Nate Perez and Rachel Waldholz, MPR News

item.title: Trump declares energy emergency
item.description: President Trump has declared an energy emergency, which his team has said would unlock additional powers to jumpstart production.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Trump signs executive order to pause TikTok ban, provide immunity to tech firms
item.description: The order follows TikTok going dark for about 14 hours after the Supreme Court upheld a law prohibiting the service from operating in the U.S. unless it breaks away from its parent company in China.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Senate confirms Marco Rubio as secretary of state, giving Trump the first member of his Cabinet
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate quickly confirmed Marco Rubio as secretary of state Monday, voting unanimously to give President Donald Trump the first member of his new Cabinet on Inauguration Day.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump seeks to end telework for federal workers
item.description: President Trump has signed an executive action ordering federal agencies to bring their workers back to the office full time. Roughly 1.1 million federal employees are telework-eligible.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: Nevada Democrats congratulate, caution Trump as he starts 2nd term
item.description: Southern Nevada’s congressional delegation -- all Democrats -- congratulated and cautioned President Donald Trump as he began his second term Monday.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump to issue 10 executive orders cracking down on border
item.description: President-elect Trump is set to sign off on 10 executive orders Monday, moving swiftly to crack down on immigration and drug cartels during his first day in office with a blitzkrieg of immigration actions.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Trump inauguration to kick off 100-day rush
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is set to be sworn in at the Capitol on Monday, solidifying his stunning return to the White House and kicking off what is expected to be a busy 100-day rush focused on a handful of hot-button issues.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: Trump to promise 'new era of national success' in inaugural address
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is set to promise a “new era of national success” in his inaugural address, according to The Wall Street Journal.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Biden pardons Fauci, Jan. 6 committee ahead of Trump inauguration
item.description: (The Hill) -- President Joe Biden granted pardons to several prominent public servants Monday who have faced attacks from President-elect Donald Trump in one of his final acts in office. Biden issued pardons for Dr.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5
